title: Surgical guide for improved angulation control
abstract: A surgical guide includes a base having a first body having a proximal opening, a distal opening and an internal chamber therebetween. The proximal opening has a plurality of recesses disposed along an inner perimeter of the proximal opening. A first insert includes a second body having a proximal end, a distal end, and a channel extending therebetween. The proximal end has a plurality of protrusions disposed along an outer perimeter and configured to interface with the plurality of recesses. The channel is angled away from a central vertical axis by a first angle. A surgical guide kit and a method for guiding an external ventricular drain are also described.
claims:
Claims CLAIMS What is claimed is: 1. A surgical guide comprising: a base comprising a first body having a proximal opening, a distal opening and an internal chamber therebetween, the proximal opening having a plurality of recesses disposed along an inner perimeter of the proximal opening; and a first insert comprising a second body having a proximal end, a distal end, and a channel extending therebetween, the proximal end having a plurality of protrusions disposed along an outer perimeter and configured to interface with the plurality of recesses, the channel angled away from a central vertical axis by a first angle. 2. The surgical guide of claim 1 further comprising: a tubular structure extending proximally away from the proximal end and distally away from the distal end with the channel extending therethrough. 3. The surgical guide of claim 1 , wherein the proximal opening is circular and centered on the central vertical axis. 4. The surgical guide of claim 3, wherein the outer perimeter is circular and centered on the central vertical axis. 5. The surgical guide of claim 4, wherein the central vertical axis intersects with the channel axis in a distal plane of the base. 6. The surgical guide of claim 1 , wherein the internal chamber tapers distally. 7. The surgical guide of claim 6, wherein the second body tapers distally. 8. The surgical guide of claim 1 , wherein the internal chamber and second body taper distally at a common angle. 9. The surgical guide of claim 1 further comprising: at least three legs extending distally from the base. 10. The surgical guide of claim 9, wherein the at least three legs are spaced equally apart. 11 . The surgical guide of claim 1 , wherein the plurality of recesses each have the same dimension. 12. The surgical guide of claim 1 , wherein the plurality of recesses are spaced equally apart. 13. The surgical guide of claim 1 , wherein the plurality of recesses are spaced about 15 degrees apart. 14. The surgical guide of claim 1 , wherein the plurality of recesses are spaced between 10 and 15 degrees apart. 15. The surgical guide of claim 1 , wherein the plurality of recesses are spaced between 5 and 10 degrees apart. 16. The surgical guide of claim 1 , wherein the plurality of recesses are spaced between 3 degrees and 20 degrees apart. 17. The surgical guide of claim 1 , wherein the plurality of protrusions each have the same dimension. 18. The surgical guide of claim 1 , wherein the plurality of protrusions are spaced equally apart. 19. The surgical guide of claim 1 , wherein the plurality of protrusions are spaced about 15 degrees apart. 20. The surgical guide of claim 1 , wherein the plurality of protrusions are spaced between 10 and 15 degrees apart. 21 . The surgical guide of claim 1 , wherein the plurality of protrusions are spaced between 5 and 10 degrees apart. 22. The surgical guide of claim 1 , wherein the plurality of protrusions are spaced between 3 degrees and 20 degrees apart. 23. A surgical guide kit comprising: the surgical guide of claim 1 ; and a second insert comprising a third body having a proximal end, a distal end, and a channel extending therebetween, the proximal end having a plurality of protrusions disposed along an outer perimeter and configured to interface with the plurality of recesses, the channel angled away from a central vertical axis by a second angle. 24. The surgical guide kit of claim 23 further comprising: a third insert comprising a third body having a proximal end, a distal end, and a channel extending therebetween, the proximal end having a plurality of protrusions disposed along an outer perimeter and configured to interface with the plurality of recesses, the channel angled away from a central vertical axis by a third angle. 25. A method for guiding a device comprising: providing the kit of claim 24; imaging a patientâs head; 

 determining an insertion parameter based on the imaging; selecting one of the first, second or third insert based on the determined insertion parameter; and inserting the selected insert into the base at a rotated position based on the determined insertion parameter. 26. The method of claim 25, wherein determining an insertion parameter includes determining a coronal adjustment angle and sagittal adjustment angle. 27. The method of claim 26, wherein determining further comprises comparing the coronal adjustment angle and the sagittal adjustment angle to predetermined values.
